title: Sulcolluma shadhbana
taxonomy: Sulcolluma shadhbana is a species of succulent plant that belongs to the family Aizoaceae. This family is commonly known as the vygies or ice plants and is native to southern Africa. Within the Aizoaceae family, Sulcolluma shadhbana is classified as a genus of flowering plants, specifically a member of the tribe Ruschieae. The classification of Sulcolluma shadhbana is further defined by its unique characteristics and growth habits. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Sulcolluma shadhbana.

The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which may aid in wound healing and skin care. Additionally, Sulcolluma shadhbana has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Its potential therapeutic benefits may also extend to the treatment of various skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential applications in modern medicine.
